title: Rounding a Decimal to the Nearest Tenth: 3.847

steps.instruction: Find the digit in the tenths place.
steps.explanation: The tenths place is the first digit after the decimal point.

steps.instruction: Check the digit immediately to the right of the rounding place.
steps.explanation: The digit to the right of the rounding place determines whether we round up or stay the same.

steps.instruction: If the deciding digit is 5 or greater, round up. If it is less than 5, round down (keep the same).
steps.work: The deciding digit is 4, which is less than 5, so we round down. The tenths digit stays 8.
steps.explanation: Since 4 < 5, the tenths digit does not increase.

steps.instruction: Drop all digits after the rounding place.
steps.work: 3.847 rounded to the nearest tenth is 3.8.
steps.explanation: We keep everything up to and including the tenths place and remove the rest.

title: Rounding a Decimal to the Nearest Hundredth: 12.6539

steps.instruction: Find the digit in the hundredths place.
steps.explanation: The hundredths place is the second digit after the decimal point.

steps.instruction: Check the digit immediately to the right of the hundredths place.
steps.explanation: This digit determines whether the hundredths digit rounds up or stays.

steps.work: 3 < 5, so we round down. The hundredths digit stays at 5.
steps.explanation: The thousandths digit is less than 5, so the hundredths place is unchanged.

steps.instruction: Keep digits through the hundredths place and drop the rest.
steps.work: 12.6539 rounded to the nearest hundredth is 12.65.
steps.explanation: We retain the ones, tenths, and hundredths digits and remove everything after.
